FG_DynamicLoraLoader.description:
Dynamic LoRA stacker with optional CLIP entry.
 - Can be used with SDXL models. (CLIP required)
 - FLUX models. (CLIP optional)

model.name: 🤖 Model
model.tooltip: 🤖 Model - Diffusion Model input used for generation

clip.name: 📒 Clip
clip.tooltip: 📒 Clip: used for text encoding and conditional generation. This is optional.

0.name: 🤖 Model
0.tooltip: 🤖 Model - Diffusion Model input used for generation

1.name: 📒 Clip
1.tooltip: 📒 Clip: used for text encoding and conditional generation. This is optional.

FG_CLIPTextEncode.description:
Enhanced CLIP Text Encoder. Encodes text prompts using a CLIP model into an embedding that can be used to guide the diffusion model towards generating specific images.

Many SDXL models will use a negative prompt. However, newer models appear to have better (not perfect) prompt adherence. Hence a negative prompt is not always required.

clip.name: 📒 Clip
clip.tooltip: 📒 Clip: The CLIP model used for encoding the text.

prompt_option.name: 🗒️ Prompt Zero Out
prompt_option.tooltip: 🗒️ Switch this to override the negative prompt; zeroing out it's weight.

vae.name: 🔣 VAE
vae.tooltip: 🔣 VAE: Used to encode the reference images into latent pixels that the model understands.

image1.name: 🖼️ Picture 1
image1.tooltip: 🖼️ Picture 1 — Reference Image 1, used for conditional generation and latent space encoding.

image2.name: 🖼️ Picture 2
image2.tooltip: 🖼️ Picture 2 — Reference Image 2, used for conditional generation and latent space encoding.

image3.name: 🖼️ Picture 3
image3.tooltip: 🖼️ Picture 3 — Reference Image 3, used for conditional generation and latent space encoding.

vl_based_clip.name: 📃 Show VL Textbox
vl_based_clip.tooltip: 📃 Toggle to show the Visual Learning textbox.. this does not disable it, just hides it.

vl_instruction.name: ✏️ Visual Learning Instructions
vl_instruction.tooltip: ✏️ System instructions used to guide the editing of reference images.

0.name: ➕ Positive Conditioning
0.tooltip: Attach this to the + side of the KSampler

1.name: ➖ Negative Conditioning
1.tooltip: Attach this to the - side of the KSampler

FG_ModelReferenceLatentMethod.description: This node controls how reference images are injected into the FLUX Kontext diffusion process. This is best used if reference images are passed with a VAE, that way, the VAE-encoded images get attached to the conditioning as reference_latents. This node tells the model how to use them during generation.

positive_conditioning.name: Positive Conditioning
positive_conditioning.tooltip: ➕ Positive prompt conditioning info from CLIP.

negative_conditioning.name: Negative conditioning
negative_conditioning.tooltip: ➖ Negative prompt conditioning info from CLIP.

positive_reference_latent_method.name: + latent method option
positive_reference_latent_method.tooltip: The method the model will use to interpret the positive image latents.

negative_reference_latent_method.name: - latent method option
negative_reference_latent_method.tooltip: The method the model will use to interpret the negative image latents.

FG_ImageScaler.description: Scale an image to a predetermined multiple. Among other things.

vae.name: 🔣 VAE
vae.tooltip: (Optional) Variational AutoEncoder: Used for encoding the image and mask into a latent.

round_to_multiple.name: Ⓜ️ Rounding Multiple
round_to_multiple.tooltip: What to base your scaled image dimesnions on.

desired_width.name: 📐 Desired Width
desired_width.tooltip: Enter if you want to keep exact dimensions.

desired_height.name: 📏 Height
desired_height.tooltip: Enter if you want to keep exact dimensions.

base_model.name: 🧮 Base Model
base_model.tooltip: Default is SDXL but for an empty latent, you need to pick FLUX for FLUX models as they produce 16 channel empty latents.

2.name: 🏞️ Latent
2.tooltip: Empty latent if no VAE is provided. Encoded latent image if VAE is provided. Encoded inpaint latent image if both VAE and Mask is provided.

3.name: 📐 Width
3.tooltip: The width of the output image.

4.name: 📏 Height
4.tooltip: The height of the output image.

FG_Advanced_KSampler.description:
Integrated KSampler for SDXL + FLUX models. Supports:
 - Optimized AuraFlow shift
 - Optimized CFG normalization adjustment

model.name: 🤖 Model
model.tooltip: 🤖 Model - Diffusion model input used as the core model for image generation

latent_image.name: 🏞️ Latent input.
latent_image.tooltip: 🏞️ Either an empty latent or encoded pixels from a VAE

compute_sigmas.name: 🗿 Sigma
compute_sigmas.tooltip: 🗿 Typically done internally, however, for more control, turn on. Use with ip adapters or controlnet. No need to compute sigmas with empty latent images.

add_noise.name: ✨ Add Noise
add_noise.tooltip: Enabled by default to always add noise to the latent image. Disable if you do not want to denoise further.

noise_seed.name: 🎲 Seed
noise_seed.tooltip: 🎲 The random seed for generating noise.

cfg.name: 🎛️ Classifier Free Guidance
cfg.tooltip: 🎛️ Used to balance randomness and prompt adherence. Increasing this value makes the results align more closely with the prompt, but setting it too high may lead to a decline in image quality.

sampler_name.name: 🌀 Sampler
sampler_name.tooltip: 🌀 Sampling algorithms influence result quality, generation speed, and stylistic characteristics.

scheduler.name: 📈 Scheduler
scheduler.tooltip: 📈 A method for controlling the gradual removal of noise.

start_at_step.name: 🚩 Starting Step
start_at_step.tooltip: Percantage at which to begin denoising

end_at_step.name: 🏁 Finishing Step
end_at_step.tooltip: Percantage at which to finish denoising

denoise.name: 🔄 Denoise
denoise.tooltip: 🔄 The percantage of noise to be removed.

auraflow_shift.name: ⚡ Model Sampling Aura Flow Shift
auraflow_shift.tooltip: ⚡ AuraFlow model sampling shift parameter affecting speed and quality (0-100). Leave at Zero to skip applying.

cfg_norm_strength.name: ⚖️ CFGNorm Strength
cfg_norm_strength.tooltip: ⚖️ CFG Normalization strength, dynamically adjusts CFG guidance strength (0-100). Leave at Zero to skip applying

return_with_leftover_noise.name: Return w/leftover noise ➜
return_with_leftover_noise.tooltip: Turn on if you intend to daisy chain samplers.
